paper: Corporate and Other Laws  |  Master Test 03
_placement: Hardik's scan is A4, tilted, with no usable left margin, so step marks sit in the RIGHT margin. Every x/y below was found by searching the rendered page for a genuinely blank rectangle - not eyeballed. Page 5 has no blank band anywhere, so its note lives on the feedback page instead.

steps.for: scheme 1 - s.139(2), two terms of five years
(e) office address in India - ceiling reached at 5
scheme 1 - s.9 GCA, exclude first include last
scheme 1 - s.6 stated and applied to the facts
scheme 1+2 - the 1%/USD 5m rule and 1% of 12m = 120,000, both in one sentence
scheme 5 - limit given but never concludes approval is required
Beneficial Construction - meaning/purpose and liberal nature. One mark covers both: he wrote flowing prose, not two discrete points

_notes: This scan has no room to place comments beside the answers without covering the handwriting, so the copy carries marks only and all reasoning is on the REMARKS page.

remarks.title: Part I
lines: 7 of 8 correct. Only Q8 wrong - you
chose (D), the key is (B). Mr John CAN
invest USD 150,000 under the FDI automatic
route; it is Mr Christopher who cannot
remit without RBI approval.

remarks.title: Auditor rotation / common partner
lines: Earned: Sec 139(2) two-terms rule, the
common-partner bar, the 5 year cooling
off, and the conclusion that Mehta cannot
be appointed. All correct.
Not earned: the question asks WHEN the
restriction is lifted. The answer is
2032 (2027 + 5). You never write it.

remarks.title: Foreign company - documents u/s 380
lines: Excellent. Seven of the eight documents,
plus the 30 days and Form FC-1.
The ceiling is 1 mark per point up to 5,
so 5 awarded - you wrote more than needed.
Only missing: the declaration that no
director is convicted or debarred.

remarks.title: Dividend dates / General Clauses Act
lines: Full marks. Both ranges exactly right:
28 Sep to 27 Oct, and 28 Oct to 3 Nov,
both days inclusive, and you applied
"from" excludes / "to" includes correctly.
Warning: you name the Acts but never the
sections - Sec 9 of the GCA, Sec 127 and
Sec 124. Marks given.

remarks.title: Beneficial vs Exceptional Construction
lines: Earned: the meaning and purpose of
Beneficial Construction - liberal reading
that favours the class the statute
protects.
Not earned: the question says "write
any four DIFFERENCES". You described
each rule separately instead of
contrasting them - use a table.
Also, your Rule of Exceptional Construction
is not what the model means. Here it
is the NARROW reading of provisions.

remarks.title: LLP with a single partner / winding up
lines: Full marks and your best answer. Sec 6
stated and applied to the quarrel, and
all five grounds for winding up by the
Tribunal reproduced correctly.
Warning: Sec 6 and Sec 64 are never named.

remarks.title: FEMA - donations and remittances
lines: Earned: the 1% / USD 5m rule, 1% of
12,000,000 = 120,000, the conclusion
that 120,000 may be donated without
approval, and the 5% / USD 100,000 rule.
Half earned: (2) asks "does it need RBI
approval?" You never answer. Say yes,
prior approval is required.

remarks.title: When interpretation is applied
lines: Earned: ambiguity in language, and vague
or unclear wording.
Not earned: five more circumstances are
listed - conflict, silence/omission,
changing conditions, technical language,
and external events/agreements.
You explained WHY interpretation is
needed. The question asked WHEN it applies.

footer: If you want to go deeper on any of this, you can connect with an SME.
